title: Emotional scenes as family performs last rites of Twisha Sharma 12 days after her death – video
content: Twisha Sharma's funeral was held in Bhopal as her family continues to demand justice in the alleged dowry death case. The Supreme Court has taken suo motu cognisance of the matter, citing institutional bias, while a second post-mortem is being conducted. Her husband, the prime accused, has been arrested and remanded to police custody.

title: Iran secretly bought Chinese satellite tech via UAE — then struck Abu Dhabi: Report
content: Leaked records reveal Iran's Revolutionary Guards secretly acquired advanced Chinese satellite communication equipment via a UAE-based procurement network. The technology, crucial for Tehran's drone and missile programs, was shipped through Dubai, highlighting sophisticated methods to bypass Western sanctions and reach Iran's military apparatus.

title: Jeff Bezos to Amazon users: We can’t run Amazon the way NYC runs its school system
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has sharply criticized New York City's public school spending, likening its inefficiencies to a poorly run business. He argued that substantial funds aren't reaching teachers, despite high per-student costs. Bezos also dismissed 'tax-the-rich' policies, advocating for identifying and solving root causes of economic struggles instead.

title: Mumbai Indians bring back rare 11-year-old strategy vs Rajasthan Royals
content: Mumbai Indians tried a new tactic in their final league match. They used three overs of spin in the Powerplay against Rajasthan Royals. This unusual strategy was also seen against Lucknow Super Giants earlier in IPL 2026. It is a rare move for the team in their IPL history. Mumbai Indians have had a mixed season in IPL 2026.

title: Watch: Childhood friends turn rivals, ram vehicles in violent road rage over woman in Vijayawada
content: Childhood friends and distant relatives, Lingamaneni Rohit and Bolla Om, engaged in a violent car confrontation in Vijayawada over a woman. The dispute escalated from WhatsApp messages to ramming vehicles and using weapons, causing panic. Police took both into custody, suspecting ganja influence, with initial tests showing Om positive.

title: CEO Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta staff has 2 promises for survivors
content: Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta employees on May 20 doubled as a message to the 70,000 staff still standing. The Meta CEO made two promises: no further company-wide layoffs in 2026, and better communication after weeks of internal anxiety. The memo lands as Meta funnels $125-145 billion into AI infrastructure, shifts 7,000 staff to AI projects, and cancels 6,000 open roles.

title: 'Mungerilal ke haseen sapne': CM Fadnavis mocks Rahul's prediction of NDA's fall
content: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis dismissed Rahul Gandhi's prediction of the NDA government's collapse as "daydreaming," comparing it to a popular television serial. Fadnavis made these remarks while inaugurating a logistics park in Nagpur, highlighting its potential to boost the manufacturing sector and strengthen the city's position as a logistics hub.

title: When and where will banks remain closed this week? Check state-wise list
content: Bank customers across India should note extended holiday closures from May 26-28, 2026, impacting numerous states for national and religious observances like Kazi Nazrul Islam's birth anniversary and Eid-ul-Adha.  Remember, second and fourth Saturdays also mean branch shutdowns. Plan ahead for essential services, as digital banking remains fully operational.

title: Karnataka river tragedy: 7 women among 8 dead in Bhatkal mishap
content: Tragedy struck Karnataka's Uttara Kannada district on Sunday as at least eight individuals, predominantly women, lost their lives after being swept away while bathing in the Thatte Hakkalu river near Bhatkal. A massive search and rescue operation is underway, with authorities concerned the grim toll may escalate. Further updates are anticipated as the situation unfolds.

title: How US govt’s chip shortage problem may be ‘good news' for Anthropic
content: Facing a severe computer chip shortage, US intelligence agencies are increasing their reliance on AI firm Anthropic, despite its blacklisting as a national security threat. A $9 billion emergency fund aims to secure advanced semiconductors for AI models on classified networks, crucial for sifting through vast data and countering foreign adversaries like China.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi flop show triggers viral Hardik Pandya reaction
content: Deepak Chahar provided Mumbai Indians with a crucial breakthrough, dismissing Rajasthan Royals' Vaibhav Sooryavanshi early in their IPL 2026 clash at Wankhede.  Sooryavanshi's dismissal, caught by Naman Dheer, sent the crowd into a frenzy. Mumbai Indians opted to bowl first in this must-win encounter for Rajasthan Royals.

title: Kangana Ranaut pens heartfelt note for veteran actress Vyjayanthimala
content: Kangana Ranaut paid a heartfelt tribute to legendary actress and dancer Vyjayanthimala on social media, sharing a vintage Bharatanatyam video and calling her "heavenly" and "born only to be worshipped." Vyjayanthimala, a Padma Vibhushan awardee, is celebrated for iconic films like 'Nagin', 'Devdas', and her legendary pairing with Dilip Kumar.

title: NFL insiders predict troubling end to Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini controversy
content: The controversy involving Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini continues to dominate NFL headlines, with analysts split on how it will end. Many believe the scandal will fade if the New England Patriots keep winning, while others think Russini’s possible public response could reignite the story. Several insiders warned the situation may permanently affect Vrabel’s reputation and place extra pressure on New England heading into the 2026 season.

title: Russia launches hypersonic Oreshnik & 3 other missiles in massive attack on Ukraine
content: Russia launched a large overnight missile and drone assault on Ukraine, reportedly using Oreshnik, Iskander, Kinzhal and Zircon missiles in retaliation for Ukrainian attacks inside Russia. Ukrainian authorities said two people were killed and 56 injured in strikes across Kyiv. President Volodymyr Zelenskyy claimed Russia used the hypersonic Oreshnik missile, causing widespread damage to homes, schools and infrastructure.

title: From anti-India racism to visa issues - Key takeaways from Rubio-Jaishankar joint press conference
content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and external affairs minister S Jaishankar reaffirmed strong US-India strategic ties, highlighting cooperation in defence, trade, energy and technology. Rubio said US visa reforms were global, not India-specific, while condemning racist remarks against Indian Americans. Both leaders discussed energy security, Quad cooperation, trade negotiations, Iran tensions and India’s multi-alignment foreign policy approach.

title: Indiramma housing scheme phase 2: Telangana approves construction of 2.5 lakh houses
content: The state of Telangana is making a significant leap in housing infrastructure by initiating the second phase of the Indiramma scheme, targeting the establishment of 250,000 homes to replace dilapidated kutcha houses. Additionally, the government is stepping in to support ongoing constructions, with financial assistance reaching up to Rs 3 lakh for structures still at the foundation phase.

title: Indo-US defence ties: Jaishankar pushes ‘Make in India’ approach in talks with Rubio
content: India and the US are prioritizing 'Make in India' in defense cooperation, following recent talks between external affairs minister S Jaishankar and US secretary of State Marco Rubio. Discussions also covered energy security, trade, and advancements in critical technologies like semiconductors and AI, underscoring a deepening strategic alliance.

title: Amid heatwave, tourists rush to hills; Manali sees severe traffic congestion
content: Manali experienced severe traffic congestion late Saturday night due to a surge in tourist arrivals. Long vehicle queues formed across the hill town, leaving many commuters and tourists stranded for hours. The slow traffic movement, attributed to the weekend rush, caused significant inconvenience.

title: Blogger held for 'using Shiv idol reel to promote eatery'
content: A 25-year-old food influencer, Anas Ahmed, found himself in hot water in Muzaffarnagar after right-wing activists lodged a complaint. They claimed that a short promotional video for a local eatery, which featured imagery of a Shiv idol, was offensive to religious sentiments.

title: ‘Didn’t give money for drugs’: Woman, teen son stabbed to death in Delhi
content: A shocking double murder in Govindpuri, Delhi, has led to the arrest of the victim's cousin. Police apprehended Saurabh Sahu after a brief shootout. He allegedly confessed to killing his cousin and her son due to drug addiction and resentment over financial matters. The teen was killed trying to intervene.

title: 24 killed as explosives-laden car rams into train carrying Pakistan military personnel
content: At least 24 Pakistani army personnel were killed and more than 100 injured after a suicide vehicle bomb targeted a military shuttle train in Quetta, Balochistan, on Sunday. The blast derailed a bogie, sparked a fire and damaged nearby homes and vehicles. The banned Baloch Liberation Army’s Majeed Brigade claimed responsibility, saying army personnel were the intended target.

title: After 12 days, Twisha Sharma’s last rites to be held in Bhopal today at 5pm
content: Twisha Sharma's final rites are set for 5 PM in Bhopal, a decision made due to heat and medical concerns, her cousin stated.  A second post-mortem is underway by AIIMS doctors following a High Court order.  Her husband, Samarth Singh, arrested after absconding, is in police custody.  The Supreme Court will hear the case Monday.

title: 'Thank the court': BJP leader welcomes HC relief for Vinesh Phogat in WFI dispute
content: The Delhi high court’s order came after the Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) had declared Vinesh ineligible for domestic events till June 26. Babita, herself a former wrestler, said the court’s decision was correct.

title: Cockroach Janata Party founder’s house in Maharashtra gets police protection
content: Police protection has been deployed outside the residence of Abhijeet Dipke in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar after the online rise of the Cockroach Janata Party. Police said the move was precautionary due to growing public attention, not threats. The group recently gained popularity through memes and commentary on unemployment, exam paper leaks and education issues. Dipke also alleged its social media accounts and website were compromised.

title: 'We will not stop': Rahul vows more NEET protests until education minister resigns
content: Rahul Gandhi demands Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the NEET-UG 2026 controversy, vowing continued protests until a foolproof system prevents future paper leaks. The NSUI also called for the minister's resignation and an impartial investigation, as the NTA claims only some questions, not the full paper, were leaked.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 67: Can Ranveer Singh film net Rs 1150 crore this weekend?
content: Aditya Dhar's historic spy thriller 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge', starring Ranveer Singh, enters its final theatrical stretch on Day 67. With an official JioHotstar domestic OTT release date locked for June 4, 2026, the box office juggernaut is racing to secure its next massive milestone. The film currently sits just Rs 3.65 crore away from crossing the monumental Rs 1150 crore net domestic mark in India. Fans looking forward to the high-octane action can also gear up for its dual-platform digital release next month, which features the unfiltered "Raw & Undekha" cut.

title: 30-year membership wait, ‘green-card’ system: Inside Delhi's elite Gymkhana Club
content: The Centre has ordered the historic Delhi Gymkhana Club to vacate its 27.3-acre Lutyens' Delhi premises by June 5, citing urgent need for defence infrastructure and public security. This move, stemming from governance disputes and alleged mismanagement, has shocked members and sparked debate over elite colonial clubs on prime government land.

title: Iran hits back at Rubio’s ‘energy hostage’ remark, blames US sanctions for market turmoil
content: Iran has rejected US Secretary of State Marco Rubio's remarks, accusing Washington of distorting regional realities and diverting attention from its destabilizing policies. Tehran asserts that US sanctions, not its own actions, have disrupted global energy markets and held them hostage. Rubio had previously accused Iran of holding the energy market hostage during his visit to India.

title: Canadian GP: Russell snatches pole as Antonelli, McLaren and Ferrari up for fierce battle
content: George Russell clinched pole for the Canadian Grand Prix, just after winning the Sprint race. His Mercedes teammate, championship leader Andrea Kimi Antonelli, starts second. McLaren and Ferrari are pushing hard, while Red Bull faces challenges. An intense intra-team rivalry between Russell and Antonelli adds further drama to the highly anticipated race.

title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is not giving up on China and the reason is next-gen CPUs
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang confirmed China remains crucial for the company's $1 trillion sales forecast, including a new $200 billion CPU market. Despite US-China tensions, Huang emphasized serving the vital Chinese market. Nvidia is also ramping up production for its next-generation 'Vera' CPU and GPU platform, aiming to capture the burgeoning agentic AI sector.

title: 'That is the bottom line': Rohit Sharma reveals big India dream
content: Rohit Sharma expressed his ambition for India to become the undisputed powerhouse of world cricket, citing recent successes across formats and age groups as evidence of strong progress. He also noted the game's evolution, particularly in white-ball cricket, with a more fearless and aggressive batting approach now setting new standards.

title: China’s 'super carrier' push: World's largest naval support ship under construction
content: China is reportedly nearing the launch of its largest naval support vessel, a massive ship designed to bolster its growing blue-water capabilities. Images reveal significant construction progress, suggesting a potential completion by year-end and service entry between 2027-2028. This development underscores China's expanding naval ambitions.

title: UP woman ends life after video call with husband; family alleges harassment
content: A 25-year-old woman allegedly died by suicide after an argument with her husband over a video call. Her family claims she was harassed by her husband and in-laws, leading to her returning to her parental home. Police are investigating the allegations of harassment and abetment to suicide.

title: India’s fuel demand growth may slow sharply in H2 2026 amid price hikes, austerity push
content: India's transportation fuel demand growth is set for a sharp slowdown in late 2026. Higher prices, government conservation efforts, and a weakening rupee are impacting mobility. Analysts have significantly cut growth forecasts, with petrol demand facing the steepest decline due to reduced commuting and discretionary travel.

title: What is Trump's 'America First' visa policy? Here's what it means for Indians
content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced a new 'America First' visa scheduling system in India, prioritizing business professionals and investors crucial to US economic and strategic interests. This initiative aims to strengthen bilateral ties and expedite travel for those contributing to trade and investment, while also emphasizing stricter security screenings for all applicants.

title: Hyderabad lawyer fighting waqf property cases dies after Scorpio rams him outside home; watch
content: An advocate fighting waqf property encroachment cases was fatally run over by an SUV in Hyderabad. His son alleges two city educationists are behind the murder, citing previous threats and attacks. Police have formed special teams to investigate the incident.

title: Market recap: 6 of top-10 most-valued firms add over Rs 74,000 crore
content: India's top companies saw a significant market valuation surge last week, with Reliance Industries leading the gains. Despite a volatile trading period, the BSE Sensex managed a modest advance. While several giants added billions, others like Bharti Airtel and Hindustan Unilever experienced notable dips. Analysts point to global optimism and domestic buying as drivers for the market's recovery.

title: 'Obsession with our country's most cherished structure': Trump reacts to White House shooting
content: US President Donald Trump praised the Secret Service and law enforcement for their swift action in neutralizing a gunman near the White House. The suspect, identified as Nasire Best, was killed after firing at agents. Trump highlighted the incident's importance for future presidential security, referencing a previous security breach.

title: K.C. Venugopal slams Centre, says no government to address people’s issues in the country
content: AICC general secretary warns of rising youth anger, says unemployment and price rise remain unaddressed

title: Congress factions in Kerala ‘wishcasting’ for their respective loyalists as KPCC president’s post comes up for grabs
content: A Congress insider says AICC seems resolved to prevent current jockeying for KPCC chief’s post from backsliding into a self-destructive circular firing squad at a crucial time when ‘Team-UDF’ seeks to cast LDF, still smarting from its historic defeat, as the alliance in disarray

title: Steps will be taken to modernise prison administration: T.N. Law Minister Nirmalkumar
content: Many remand prisoners continue to remain in prison beyond the stipulated period, and the Law Department would explore the possibilities of extending legal aid to them, he said.

title: Kerala seeks urgent intervention over CBSE Plus Two evaluation complaints
content: Complaints over evaluation of CBSE Class XII examination papers under newly introduced OSM system, in which answer sheets are scanned and digitally evaluated instead of being physically corrected by teachers

title: Ladakh L-G Saxena launches ecological restoration project to reclaim 800 acres of barren land
content: Lieutenant-Governor Vinai Kumar Saxena initiates an ecological restoration project to reclaim 800 acres of barren land in Ladakh

title: Twisha Sharma death case: final rites to be held in Bhopal after second autopsy by AIIMS Delhi team
content: The Sharma family reiterated that it has cooperated fully with the investigation and continues to place faith in the legal process

title: Arms and explosives recovered in Chhattisgarh’s Kanker district
content: During the search, the forces recovered a concealed Naxal dump containing a country-made BGL launcher, 25 BGL shells, four improvised fire cartridges, empty cartridges of AK-47 and SLR rifles, around five kilograms of gunpowder

title: T.N. Speaker Prabhakar says decision on AIADMK split will show his neutrality
content: Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly Speaker J.C.D. Prabhakar paid tributes to former Speaker Si.Pa. Adithanar in Chennai on the late leader’s death anniversary on May 24, 2026

title: Overseas travel spending by Indians falls to $1.09 billion in March
content: With lesser overseas travel, foreign exchange outgo will reduce, and that can help in curbing rupee depreciation

title: ‘Every country in world has stupid people’: Rubio on racist remarks against Indians in U.S.
content: The U.S. Secretary of State also stated that the U.S. is modernising its migration system and changes in immigration policy were “not India-specific”

title: Ganga Expressway to cut freight time, unlock ₹30,000 crore annual savings on logistics
content: The six-lane expressway, expandable to eight lanes, connects Meerut to Prayagraj through 12 districts, and is being projected as a major logistics and industrial corridor linking western and eastern U.P.

title: India, U.S. looking at firming up trade deal soon
content: Secretary of State made the remarks after holding wide-ranging talks with Jaishankar that focused on boosting two-way cooperation in areas of trade, critical minerals, energy and defence

title: TDP says ‘Cockroach Janata Party’ movement reflects youth concerns, should reach policymakers
content: Palla Srinivasa Rao says the satirical online movement ‘Cockroach Janata Party’ signals youth frustration over jobs and urges young people to stay careful online

title: Rahul Gandhi trying to create ‘anarchy’ to topple Modi Government: BJP
content: Sambit Patra claimed Mr. Gandhi's "plan" was exposed after Congress leader reportedly said at a meeting of the advisory council of the Congress' Minority Department that the Modi Government will fall within a year

title: Bakrid holiday in Kerala on May 27 and 28
content: Government had declared holiday for Bakrid on May 27. However, as Bakrid is being celebrated in Kerala on May 28, decision is taken to extend the holiday

title: Marco Rubio in India LIVE: India-U.S. relations 'have not lost momentum'; hope to seal trade deal soon, says Rubio
content: U.S. Secretary of State, who held a meeting with Jaishankar, described the first day of his visit as "fantastic"

title: Avalanche hits Kanchan Ganga near Badrinath, no casualties reported
content: Avalanche near Badrinath causes no casualties or damage; officials confirm traffic and daily life remain unaffected

title: BJP criticises TVK government over law and order situation in Tamil Nadu
content: Mr. Nagenthran charged that the Chief Minister failed to ensure the law and order situation in the State and was instead focusing on publicity and propaganda and it reflected his “inexperience.”

title: Telangana nears record rabi paddy procurement, government buys 50 LMT from farmers as on May 23
content: State has already released ₹8,749.82 crore towards minimum support price (MSP) payments, accounting for 73% of the total dues

title: Srisailam temple EO inspects Annaprasadam distribution
content: The tickets for Sri Swamyvaru’s Sparsha Darshan, Sheeghra Darshan, Ati Sheeghra Darshan, and Arjitha Sevas can also be obtained through the State government’s ‘Mana Mitra’ WhatsApp service, the temple EO said

title: Congress will not rest till Education Minister Pradhan resigns: Rahul Gandhi on NEET paper leak row
content: He shared a video of students protesting in Hyderabad, highlighting the NEET paper leak and demanding Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation

title: ‘Sankey Tank is Dying’: No longer just a lake issue, say environmental groups against unscientific developments
content: During the campaign, environment groups and citizens demanded an immediate independent water quality audit and urgent intervention to stop sewage entry

title: IAF Chief A.P. Singh bats for self-reliance in defence capabilities
content: Air Chief Marshal A.P. Singh emphasises ‘Atmanirbharta’ as a strategic necessity for developing resilient indigenous defence capabilities

title: Falta repoll results LIVE: BJP's Debangshu Panda maintains massive lead over CPI(M) candidate
content: Trinamool Congress nominee Jahangir Khan, a couple of days before the repoll, announced that he would not contest

title: Kurnool police trace missing persons using facial-recognition CCTV network, says SP
content: About 722 analytics cameras, equipped for facial recognition and number-plate reading and linked to the RTGS control room in Amaravati, have helped the district police locate missing people in Madanapalle and Tirupati

title: Maximum temperatures soar across Telangana, seven Districts cross 46℃
content: Dummugudem in Bhadradri Kothagudem and Dharmapuri in Jagtial were the hottest locations in the State recording a maximum temperature of 46.3℃

title: 2008 railway exam assault: Raj Thackeray's role in instigating attack not proved, says court
content: CD containing the alleged speech of MNS chief Thackeray was never produced or proved in court, and as admitted by the investigation officer, was "left gathering dust" in the police station from 2013 to 2026, rendering it devoid of any evidentiary value, the court observed in its May 21 order

title: UPSC Prelims 2026 under way across India amid tight security
content: This year, a total of 8,19,372 candidates have enrolled for UPSC CSE 2026 for approximately 933 positions

title: Traffic diversions in Mehdipatnam for skywalk works on May 24 and 25
content: Motorists were advised to use alternate routes and follow updates issued by the Hyderabad Traffic Police on social media platforms

title: Dialogue, uninterrupted maritime trade: Jaishankar outlines India’s 5-point stand during talks with Rubio
content: Addressing a press conference, Secretary of State Marco Rubio said an announcement was possible later on a deal with Iran that could formally end the West Asia war

title: CBSE to issue refunds to students overcharged due to glitches in class 12 post-result process
content: This comes a day after Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan sought a detailed report from the CBSE over complaints of technical glitches faced by students during the re-evaluation process

title: Falta repoll: BJP leads by over 69,000 votes after 15 rounds of counting
content: "The counting is progressing peacefully. Adequate security arrangements have been made at the counting centre," a poll panel official said.

title: 200 bats die due to heatwave in Chhattisgarh’s Korba
content: Around 200 bats have died, and prima facie it appears that they succumbed to heatstroke, says Katghora Divisional Forest Officer Kumar Nishant

title: Rising prices in Tamil Nadu and a ship-sized hole in household budget
content: Fuel costs have risen steeply, thrice in the span of 10 days, as the Iran war continues to choke supplies globally. This has also led to an increase in the prices across the board, with grocery, food, and commute rates burgeoning. Many people in Tamil Nadu find the going very difficult

title: Hutti dazzles as gold prices soar in world market
content: Hutti Gold Mines, the only functional gold mine in the country, earn a whopping ₹633.34 crores more in 2025-26, thanks to skyrocketing price of gold in world market

title: Centre cracked down on CJP after IB warning on possible unrest, claim Maharashtra Congress leaders
content: Former State Congress chiefs Nana Patole and Balasaheb Thorat alleged that the government was suppressing people's anger instead of addressing issues faced by the youth, poor and unemployed

title: Degree student among four booked for dangerous late-night car racing in Hyderabad’s Knowledge City
content: According to the police, the car was being driven in a rash and dangerous manner, making heavy sounds and repeatedly taking rounds

title: Two held for allegedly duping elderly Bengaluru woman of ₹24 crore
content: (CBI). They allegedly told her that her bank accounts were linked to a money laundering case and warned her not to disclose the matter to anyone, claiming she was under “digital arrest.”

title: Revaluation process a hassle for Class 12 CBSE students in Chennai
content: Students have taken to social media to air their grievances with many noting the blurry images of their scanned copies, the half marks awarded to MCQ questions and the notification of blank pages on a written answer sheet

title: Bengaluru school headmaster arrested under Pocso Act, after minor student found pregnant
content: The accused, identified as Chandraiah, has been serving as the headmaster of a government school in Srirampura. According to police, the alleged abuse had been taking place over the past year

title: ‘Attempt to distort realities’: Iranian Embassy in India rejects Rubio’s remarks over Tehran’s nuclear programme
content: The statement was issued by Iran’s Embassy in India in response to Mr. Rubio’s remarks on talks with Iran

title: Confident of having productive talks as strong partners, says Jaishankar on meeting with Rubio
content: Secretary of State Marco Rubio emphasises that his visit is not focused on “restoring” ties, but rather on enhancing what he described as a “very solid and strong strategic partnership”

title: Delhi court orders framing charges against two CBI officials in corruption case
content: Delhi court orders corruption charges against two CBI officials for compromising investigations, while discharging others due to insufficient evidence

title: 17 officers from three services graduate from Air Force Test Pilots School
content: This year, the graduating course comprised 17 officers including 14 officers from the Indian Air Force (IAF), one officer from Indian Army and two officers from Indian Navy

title: 12 MPs, four Parliamentary panels selected for Sansad Ratna Awards
content: In a statement, the Prime Point Foundation said BJP's Jagdambika Pal (Uttar Pradesh), P.P. Chaudhary (Rajasthan), Nishikant Dubey (Jharkhand) and Shiv Sena's Shrikant Eknath Shinde (Maharashtra) are among the MPs chosen in the individual category

title: AIADMK leader Edappadi Palaniswami criticises unscheduled power cuts across Tamil Nadu
content: Mr. Palaniswami, in a statement, contended normal life was affected due to unscheduled power cuts and people had to spend sleepless nights.

title: Vizianagaram wears deserted look in heatwave; Collector advises staying indoors
content: Day temperatures touch 42°C in Vizianagaram, the Ring Road junction lies near-empty and many shops and hotels report poor business, residents staying home through the afternoon

title: Tiger kills woman in M.P.'s Bandhavgarh reserve area; angry locals ‘assault’ forest staff
content: Villagers accused forest officials of failing to respond promptly despite repeated calls for help and claimed the tiger was still roaming nearby

title: Former MP P. Venugopal quits AIADMK
content: Mr. Venugopal said the party’s general secretary (Edappadi K. Palaniswami) should use better judgment. “A person who is a Rajya Sabha MP is given a ticket to contest (Assembly polls) without thinking what will happen if he wins the MLA seat.”

title: What did the Supreme Court say about bail under UAPA?
content: What are the disagreements between recent Supreme Court rulings on UAPA bail? Why is bail so difficult under Section 43D(5) of the UAPA? What did the Court say in K.A. Najeeb about prolonged incarceration and bail? What role does Article 21 play in UAPA bail jurisprudence?

title: India is important to the U.S.’s Indo-Pacific policy, says Rubio, invites Modi to Washington
content: The U.S. Secretary of State is in India for a four-day visit to hold bilateral talks, and attend the Quad Foreign Ministers’ Meeting

title: Punjab Police ASI found dead in Amritsar
content: A police team, led by the Station House Officer of Majithia police station, reached the spot soon after the information was received. A forensic team also examined the scene, says SSP

title: West Bengal elections 2026: Who lost the most?
content: Wiped out from the North, South-west and Greater Kolkata regions in West Bengal, Trinamool has been reduced to 80 seats – its lowest since 2011

title: 88% voter turnout as Bengal’s Falta repoll remains peaceful
content: The repolling in Falta was conducted amid massive security deployment with armoured cars, drone surveillance and hundreds of Central forces on the ground

title: ITBP officials meet Kanpur top cop seeking action in medical negligence case of jawan’s mother
content: The ITBP personnel accused doctors at a private hospital of negligence that allegedly led to the amputation of his mother’s right hand; he demanded a fair investigation and strict action against the hospital administration and the doctors involved.

title: The final ‘metamorphosis’: Can cockroaches win against humans in real world?
content: cockroaches have been disliked and despised by humans since ages . but today, they are being “liked” and “followed” like never before . they would never have imagined, even in their wildest dreams, that one day would become the centre of a human movement .

title: 'Lured foreigners with fat perks': Govt says 217 Indians joined Russian army, 49 dead
content: as many as 217 Indians have joined Russian forces, of which 49 died and six are missing in action in the ongoing Russia-Ukraine war . the govt said the Indian Embassy is making efforts to bring back the 139 people released from military service .

title: Road Blocked Over Missing People, IAF Airlifts NCC Cadets From Imphal

title: Top US Diplomat Marco Rubio Was Asked About Racism Against Indians. His Reply
